params.text:
Four routes that are easy to confuse

People sometimes muddle four different things. They can overlap, but each has its own process, so it helps to recognise which one you are dealing with.

  • A concern is an informal worry raised in the moment — it can often be sorted out there and then by listening and putting it right quickly.
  • A complaint is an expression of dissatisfaction about the care or service a person has received, which needs a proper response through the complaints procedure.
  • A safeguarding issue is where an adult is being harmed, abused or neglected, or is at risk of it — this must be reported straight away through safeguarding routes, never treated as “just” a complaint.
  • Whistleblowing is a worker raising a concern in the public interest about wrongdoing or serious risk at work — it protects others, rather than resolving a personal service issue.

If you are ever unsure which route applies, do not sit on it. Report it and let it be directed to the right process. When a complaint reveals possible abuse or neglect, safeguarding always takes priority.

params.alt: Illustration for Complaint or something else?

params.title: Tap a card to flip it
params.description: Which route does each situation belong to?

dialogs.text: A resident’s son says the lunch was cold again and he is unhappy about it.
dialogs.answer: Complaint — dissatisfaction with the service received. Handle it through the complaints procedure.

dialogs.text: During a visit, someone mentions the TV remote is missing and asks if you can find it.
dialogs.answer: Concern — an informal, everyday niggle that can usually be sorted out on the spot.

dialogs.text: You notice unexplained bruising and the person flinches when a particular carer approaches.
dialogs.answer: Safeguarding issue — report immediately through safeguarding routes; do not treat it as an ordinary complaint.

dialogs.text: A carer suspects records are being falsified to hide medication errors across the service.
dialogs.answer: Whistleblowing — a public-interest concern about wrongdoing, raised through the speaking-up route.

dialogs.text: A relative is frustrated that no one returned her call about the care plan.
dialogs.answer: Complaint — dissatisfaction with the service; acknowledge it and follow the complaints process.
